sections.paragraph:
κυριεύω (s. κύριος) fut. κυριεύσω; 1 aor. ἐκυρίευσα; pf. 1 pl. κεκυριεύκαμεν; aor. pass. subj. κυριευθῇ TestJud 21:4, ptc. κυριευθείς TestAsh 1:8 (X.+) gener. ‘to be lord/master of ’

① to exercise authority or have control, rule, of persons, w. gen. of that over which rule or control is exercised (X., Mem. 2, 6, 22; Polyb. 4, 18, 2; OGI 229, 56 [III b.c.]; Gen 3:16; 37:8; En 22:14; TestJos 3:2; ApcMos 24:25; Iren. 1, 25, 3 [Harv. I 206, 11]; Jos., Bell. 1, 39; Just.) οἱ βασιλεῖς τ. ἐθνῶν κυριεύουσιν αὐτῶν Lk 22:25.—Ac 19:16 v.l. θλιβέντα κυριεῦσαι αὐτοῦ gain it through suffering B 7:11. ἐντολῆς κ. master a commandment, i.e. make it one’s own Hm 5, 2, 8. κ. τῆς πίστεώς τινος lord it over someone’s faith 2 Cor 1:24.—Of God (Theod. Da 4:25, 32; 5:21; En 22:14; EpArist 45; 269 δόξης πάσης; PGM 1, 214 ὁ κυριεύων πάντων ἀγγέλων; 12, 115 ὁ κυριεύων τοῦ παντὸς κόσμου) ὁ πάντων κυριεύων Hs 9, 23, 4. ὁ τοῦ παντὸς κόσμου κυριεύων B 21:5. Of Christ νεκρῶν καὶ ζώντων κ. Ro 14:9.—Abs. (En 103:12 οἳ κυριεύουσιν; Orig., C. Cels. 8, 4, 16; τὸ κυριεύειν Did., Gen. 113, 15) B 6:18. οἱ κυριεύοντες lords 1 Ti 6:15.

② be master of, dominate, of things that take control of a pers., transf. sense of 1 and likew. w. the gen. (Sextus 41; 363a; Philo, Leg. All. 3, 187 πάθος; TestSim 3:2, Iss 7:7): of the law κυριεύει τοῦ ἀνθρώπου Ro 7:1 (JDerrett, Law in the NT, ’70, 461–71). Of sin 6:14. Of death vs. 9.—B. 1319. DELG s.v. κύριος. M-M. TW. Spicq.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect
  2. pl. = plural

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

subj. subj. = subjunctive; subject

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

ptc. ptc. = participle

TestAsh TestAsh = Testament of Asher, s. Test12Patr—List 2

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

gener. gener. = generally

gen. gen. = genitive

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

En En = I Enoch—List 2

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

Iren. Iren. = Irenaeus, Haereses, II a.d.—List 5

Harv. Harv. = WHarvey; s. Iren.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

Hm Hm = Mandates

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Orig Orig , var. works, II–III a.d.—List 5

Did. Did. = Didymus Caecus (the Blind), commentator on OT books, IV a.d.—List 5

pers. pers. = person(s)

transf. transf. = transferred

likew. likew. = likewise

Sextus Sextus , end of II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

TestSim TestSim = Testament of Simeon, s. Test12Patr—List 2

NT NT = New Testament

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 576
